case_title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CARL ERIK LALLERSTEDT TRUST

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-09-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for Trustee.

The Court expressed that the fee amount is quite substantial. Ms. Friedeberg stated that the reality of it is that she and her Client won't be paid. The Court granted the First and Final Account and Report of Temporary Trustee; for Approval of Settlement Agreement, etc. filed by Jewish Family & Children's Services ("JFCS") (subject to review of the order). The Petition to Compel Accounting of The Carl Erick Lallerstedt Revocable Trust; for Removal of Trustee, etc. and Petition for First Accounting in Compliance with Order Compelling Account are off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition For First Accounting Of The Carl Erik Lallerstedt Revocable Trust In Compliance With Order Compelling Account Filed April 29, 2016; For Settlement Of Trust Accounting And Approval Of Trustee And Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Feb-15-2017 Continued To Apr-26-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Will Doyle and Bruce Feder for Jewish Family & Children's Services (Temporary Successor-Trustee).
Garson Soe for Andy Mchugh (Petitioner).

Mr. Feder requested a two week continuance stating that Mr. Soe provided an e-mail about four weeks ago, that they had begun working on a counter proposal which required them to do some work, and that Ms. Friedeberg was out of the Country and unable to do the counter proposal. Mr. Soe stated that the proposal was sent a month ago, that there hasn't been that much movement, and that he would like the Court to move forward and go to mediation. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than April 14, 2017. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by April 19, 2017. The First Amended Accounting, etc. and the Petition to Compel Accounting, etc. are continued to and the Parties are to return on April 26, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The Parties are to have a plan in place on how to work on the remaining issues and get the matter resolved.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jan-18-2017 Continued To Feb-15-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Authority To Pay Conservatorship Fees
Off Calendar Jan-24-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg and Will Doyle for Jewish Family & Children's Services ("JFCS").
Christophe Jew Court appointed Counsel for Eunice Julson (Beneficiary).
Garson Soe for Andy McHugh (suspended Trustee).
Rebecca Paul (Director of Fiduciary Services at JFCS) also present.

Mr. Soe stated that he has not asked his Client his position regarding removal, that some of the receipts regarding the loans and payments are fourteen years old, that he can obtain the originals from Ireland and that loans were made spontaneously. The Court informed Mr. Soe that payment of fees for having someone do the accounting comes from the Trustee's fees. Ms. Friedeberg and Ms. Paul presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to why Mr. McHugh's fee/reimbursement request should be denied and why his accounting should be continued until JFCS's accounting is filed. Ms. Friedeberg also spoke of the tremendous expense to JFCS due to delay on Mr. McHugh's part and stated that there is about $20,000.00/$25,000.00 left in the Trust. The Court suggested the Parties try and reach an agreement as soon as the hearing is over. There being no opposition, the Court granted the Petition for Authority to Pay Conservatorship Fees (subject to review of the order). Both the Amended First Accounting; Petition for Approval of Reimbursement to Trustee for Loans and Expenses and Approval of Attorney Fees, and the Petition to Compel Accounting; For Removal of Trustee, etc. are continued to February 15, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that the Parties will begin talking. If the Parties are unable to resolve the matter, the Court will refer them to Pro Bono Mediation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Barrientos. Not reported.

Petition For First Accounting Of The Carl Erik Lallerstedt Revocable Trust In Compliance With Order Compelling Account Filed April 29, 2016; For Settlement Of Trust Accounting And Approval Of Trustee And Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jan-04-2017 Continued To Jan-18-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Accounting Of The Carl Erick Lallerstedt Revocable Trust; For Removal Of Trustee; To Appoint Jewish Family And Children'S Services;
Off Calendar Jan-04-2017 Continued To Jan-18-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Garson Soe appeared by telephone for Andy McHugh.
Trisha Friedeberg and Will Doyle for Jewish Family & Children's Services ("JFCS") (Trustee).
Rebecca Paul (Director) of JFCS.

Ms. Friedeberg stated that the objections originally filed still stand and that talks that occurred early on were unproductive. Ms. Friedeberg also expressed concern regarding fees, the Trustee violating a number of duties, the Trustee withholding funds from the elderly beneficiaries and having no answers regarding the $150,000.00 loan to the Trustee. In addition, Ms. Friedeberg raised the issue of JFCS taking on a tremendous expense, stated that she would like to see a declaration regarding past conservator's fees and Mr. Jew's fees and whether the Trust can pay for them, as well as a declaration on administration of the Trust and whether Mr. Soe and the Trustee are entitled to any fees. The petition to compel an accounting, etc. and the petition for first account, etc. are continued to January 18, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Friedeberg is to file amended objections and delineation of issues no later than January 9, 2017 at noon. Mr. Soe is to file a response no later than January 12, 2017 at noon.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

Petition For First Accounting Of The Carl Erik Lallerstedt Revocable Trust In Compliance With Order Compelling Account Filed April 29, 2016; For Settlement Of Trust Accounting And Approval Of Trustee And Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Aug-22-2016 Continued To Sep-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for Jewish Family & Children's Services, represented by Rebecca Paul. Court appointed counsel Christopher Jew for Eunice Julson. Garson Soe for (former trustee) Andy McHugh.

Mr. Soe stated that they need clarification, asked if the court will accept the accounting and that if it does not they will have to break it down and will incur 6 separate filing fees. Ms. Friedeberg stated that they do not have an issue, that they just want to see the time period and confirmed that there are three current petitions which are the accounting, the petition for the Special Needs Trust (SNT) and the petition to remove the trustee, that the urgency is distribution to the SNT and that they started this over a year ago so that Ms. Julson could enjoy her inheritance. Mr. Soe reported that the concern of the former trustee is that there will not be sufficient funds to cover what has been loaned and paid out of pocket by the trustee. Mr. Jew stated that he concurs with Ms. Friedeberg. Ms. Friedeberg stated that they discussed distribution of $30,000.00 to start getting the services that Ms. Julson needs, that it is within the Court's discretion to order this distribution, that they have objections to reimbursements, that the property was not productive and that $300,000.00 was paid for renovations but was not recouped. The Court requested that Ms. Friedeberg submit a proposed order and that Mr. Soe can object. The petition for first accounting of the Carl Erik Lallerstedt revocable trust in compliance with order compelling account filed April 29, 2016, etc., and the Petition for Order Approving Distribution of Trust funds were continued to September 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on filing an amended accounting covering the two year period. Ms. Friedeberg stated that notice needs to be given to all remainder beneficiaries.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Accounting Of The Carl Erick Lallerstedt Revocable Trust; For Removal Of Trustee; To Appoint Jewish Family And Children'S Services;
Off Calendar Apr-25-2016 Continued To Jun-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg & Will Doyle for and with Jewish Family & Children's Services represented by Rebecca Paul. Garson Soe for Andy McHugh.

Ms. Friedeberg reported that she spoke with Mr. Jew on Friday, that he could not be here today, that Mr. Soe provided bank statements that need to be reviewed, that there are concerning issues that cannot be confirmed without an accounting, that there appears to be co-mingling, that the trustee has lived in the property rent free, that they want an accounting ordered, that the conservatee is on Medi-Cal and that it would be disruptive to move her to a non Medi-Cal facility, that the conservator has come up with ways to use the money for things that would benefit the conservatee, that they would need to do a pooled trustee and that the funds need to be put aside ASAP. Mr. Soe stated that he agrees with Ms. Friedeberg and that they are 70% done with the accounting. The Court stated that it needs a proposed order on the accounting setting boundaries and a time frame. Ms. Friedeberg stated that they need to file a petition for substituted judgment, that they will do so right away and will address the notice issues. The petition to compel an accounting of the Carl Erick Lallerstedt revocable trust; etc., was continued to June 6, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
A status on compliance with order on accounting is set for the same date.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
